semanticCore: Scales, balance, justice, weight, straight, or measure
quranicRelevance: Weighing with fair balance scale (Qistas - 'bil-qistasi al-mustaqeemi').

keyDerivations.meaning: Straight balance, scales of justice, fair weigh-beam, or standard measure

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَزِنُوا بِالْقِسْطَاسِ الْمُسْتَقِيمِ (17:35): And weigh with the straight/fair balance (commercial integrity and objective societal justice).
discardedNoise: Justice values (Qist) - conceptually related but from q-s-t (triliteral), whereas Qistas is quadriliteral.
summaryEn: The root represents straight balance scales (Qistas), establishing commercial integrity and fair weighing measures as fundamental to stable societies.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الميزان السوي العادل والمقاييس الشريفة (قسطاس)، آمراً بإقامة الكيل التجاري والموازين المجتمعية بلا بخس أو تلاعب (بالقسطاس المستقيم).
rawText: قسطس قُسْطَاسٌ and قِسْطَاسٌ A balance, or instrument for weighing: (S, Msb, K; and Bd in xvii. 37:) or the most even and most just kind thereof: or such as is just, of whatever kind it be: (K:) or i. q. قَبَّانٌ [a steelyard]: or, as Lth thinks, the iron of the قبّان: or i. q. شَاهِينٌ [the beam of a balance]: (TA:) or i. q. فَرَسْطُونٌ [an arabicized Persian word, signifying a public standard of weights or measures]: (Zj, TA:) also written قصطاس: (K:) said to be Arabic, from القِسْطُ, meaning “ justice: ” (Msb:) or a Greek word arabicized; (IDrd, Msb, K;) and its being so does not impugn the truth of the Kur-án's being [altogether] Arabic; for when a foreign word is used by the Arabs, and made by them conformable with their language in respect of desinential syntax and determinateness and indeterminateness and the like, it becomes Arabic: (Bd, ubi supra:) pl. قَسَاطِيسُ. (Msb.)
